claim: An authored-spec requirement's link to code shall carry two ORTHOGONAL fields, never collapsed into one: implemented_by names WHERE the requirement is EMBODIED (a path-qualified file:symbol reference into the domain's authored spec/ tree, the model/method that realizes the claim), and verified_by names WHERE the requirement is PROVEN (a path-qualified file:test reference, the executable test that demonstrates the claim holds). A symbol that embodies a requirement without a test that proves it, or a test cited without the symbol it exercises, is an incomplete SpecLink -- ENFORCED status requires both halves together (see R-enforced-requires-enforcer-or-authored-link), but the two fields answer two DIFFERENT questions and shall never be merged into a single field or treated as aliases of one another.
why: PLAN-authored-spec-discipline.md §4 ("Схема связи: requirement -> code -> test") and §12 (steward decision 2026-07-16) establish implemented_by/verified_by as two separate fields on Requirement (internal/ontology/requirement.go), landed additively in task #223 and already carrying inline doc comments naming the same embodied/proven distinction (requirement.go:80-96: "ImplementedBy names WHERE this requirement is embodied", "VerifiedBy names WHERE this requirement is PROVEN"). R-authored-spec-links-mechanically-checked (#223) and R-enforced-requires-enforcer-or-authored-link (#223) already model the MECHANICAL checking of these fields (symbol/test resolvability, non-vacuousness, no-skip, no-reuse) and the DISJUNCTIVE ENFORCED gate that accepts either enforced_by or implemented_by+verified_by together -- but neither requirement states the CONCEPTUAL distinction itself: why two fields exist, what each one MEANS, and why they are not interchangeable. Without this requirement as a first-class node, the SpecLink concept (PLAN §4's requirement->code->test schema) exists only in code comments and PLAN prose, not as a graph anchor a future agent can cite when modeling a business domain's own spec/ tree (task #224 pilot, task #236 recursion into hotam-spec-self's own engine code). This requirement is the missing OBJECT-level node: it names the SpecLink concept and its two fields' distinct semantics, one layer above the two already-landed mechanical-check requirements which govern HOW the fields are validated, not WHAT they mean. F5 remediation (2026-07-17, @fh adversarial review): the previous carrier (implemented_by internal/gate/spec_resolver.go:ResolveSpecSymbol, verified_by a symbol-resolution staleness mutation test) proved a SINGLE field resolves correctly, not that the two fields are ORTHOGONAL -- the claim's own load-bearing word. The new carrier is internal/proposal/mutate.go's resolveImplementedBy AND resolveVerifiedBy together -- two structurally near-identical wrapper functions (both delegate to coalesceSlice with the same clear-sentinel handling) that are consequently easy to cross-wire by accident, proven distinct and independently-addressable by TestApply_Requirement_ImplementedByAndVerifiedByAreOrthogonal (internal/proposal/proposal_test.go): an implemented_by-only UPDATE leaves verified_by byte-identical, a verified_by-only UPDATE leaves implemented_by byte-identical, and each field's own clear-sentinel clears only itself. This is a genuine ORTHOGONALITY proof (mutation-verified: swapping the two resolve calls in mutate.go's apply path makes the test fail), not merely a staleness/resolvability check on one field in isolation.
evidence: cd D:/ai_dev/prat/HotamSpec && go test ./internal/proposal/... -run TestApply_Requirement_ImplementedByAndVerifiedByAreOrthogonal -v : PASS (F5 remediation, 2026-07-17); MUTATION PROOF: swapping the resolveImplementedBy/resolveVerifiedBy call arguments in mutate.go's ProposedRequirement.mutate (p.ImplementedBy<->p.VerifiedBy cross-wired) makes this same test FAIL with 3 explicit orthogonality-violation messages; reverted, test green again -- proves the test has teeth against exactly the failure mode the claim's own text warns against ('never merged into a single field or treated as aliases of one another')
